context: Path:   accumulation of fluid / tissue √  Kerley A lines = relatively long fine linear shadows in upper lungs, deep within lung parenchyma radiating from hila √  Kerley B lines = short horizontally oriented peripheral lines extending + perpendicular to pleura in costophrenic angles + retrosternal clear space (b)  reticulations =  innumerable interlacing linear opacities suggesting a mesh / network √  Kerley C lines = fine "spider web / lacelike" polygonal opacities distributed primarily in a peripheral / subpleural location Path:   pulmonary fibrosis (lower lobes), hypersensitivity pneumonitis
